objective: Reduce the current mixed dirty worktree into a safe, reviewable, governed state by removing secret-bearing artifacts, reverting formatter-only noise, and separating the remaining substantive payload into clear keep versus discard buckets.

in_scope: create a formal cleanup initiative and prompt pair
remove or sanitize secret-bearing local artifacts
revert tracked formatter-only fallout from the failed repo-wide pre-commit run
classify remaining substantive payload into keep, split, and delete buckets
out_of_scope: committing the remaining substantive payload without explicit user authorization
implementing new runtime features as part of cleanup
changing commercialization scope or MA scorecard requirements
deleting substantive feature work without explicit classification

success_criteria: no plaintext credentials remain in local workflow docs
all tracked formatter-only fallout is reverted
the remaining worktree is materially smaller and easier to separate
remaining dirty files are classified into explicit keep split delete buckets

execution_status.note: created cleanup plan triplet prompt pair and execution-plan initiative entry
removed CODEX_CONFIGURATION.md and AGENT_SYSTEM_DEEP_DIVE.md from the worktree; exposed credentials require rotation
reverted tracked formatter-only fallout; formatter-noise bucket is zero
user directed treating the remaining substantive payload as intended keep-work; committed and pushed after scrubbing the push-protection-blocked Azure secret in create_teams_workspace.py
synchronized cleanup plan and governance trackers to reflect the completed ship and return focus to the active enterprise-readiness master plan
